title: The Double-Edged Sword of Tolerance: Safeguarding Open Societies
topic: The Paradox of Tolerance in a Free Society

questions.sentence: This paradox posits that unlimited tolerance must lead to the disappearance of tolerance itself

If an intolerant group actively propagates doctrines that deny the rights and freedoms of others or worse incites violence and oppression then a tolerant society has a moral imperative to defend itself against such forces
The challenge for a free society then is to delineate the precarious line between protecting individual liberties and safeguarding the collective good
The resolution to this paradox does not lie in abandoning tolerance but in redefining it as an active rather than passive virtue
Ultimately grappling with the paradox of tolerance is a continuous nuanced struggle inherent to the maintenance of a free society
